'''Noah Booth''' (born 21 October 2004) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at [[Hunslet RLFC|Hunslet]] in [[2024 RFL League One|League One]] and the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Booth was born in [[Castleford, Yorkshire]], England. His father [[Craig Booth]] is a former professional rugby league footballer.<ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/hull-kr-rookies-unbelievable-magic-1133921 Hull KR rookie's 'unbelievable' Magic Weekend debut came after nervy phone call]</ref>

He played for [[Lock Lane ARLFC]] as a junior.<ref>[https://hullkr.co.uk/news/noah-booth-signs-for-robins-in-wakefield-swap-deal Noah Booth signs for Robins in Wakefield Swap Deal]</ref><ref>[https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-noah-booth-wakefield-10284270 Hull KR youngster's words of praise for 'brilliant' Wakefield Trinity]</ref>

Booth was in the Yorkshire Origin squad at youth level.<ref>[https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2023/04/17/six-academy-players-named-in-yorkshire-origin-squad/ Six Academy Players Named In Yorkshire Origin Squad]</ref>

He came through the [[Castleford Tigers]] system, before joining the [[Warrington Wolves]] Academy in 2021.<ref>[https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinitys-young-star-noah-booth-speaks-of-dream-come-true-as-he-commits-to-belle-vue-club-4785401 Wakefield Trinity's young star Noah Booth speaks of 'dream come true' as he commits to Belle Vue club]</ref>

==Career==
Booth joined [[Wakefield Trinity]] ahead of the [[2024 Super League season]].<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/warrington-wolves-fullback-seemingly-joins-new-club-ahead-of-2024-campaign-87695/ Rugby League NewsWarrington Wolves fullback seemingly joins new club ahead of 2024 campaign]</ref> He made his [[Wakefield Trinity|Trinity]] debut in the [[1895]] against the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in February 2024.

He spent time in 2024 on loan at [[Hunslet RLFC|Hunslet]] in [[2024 RFL League One|League One]].<ref>[https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-loan-out-noah-booth-to-championship-new-boys-hunslet-4872311 Wakefield Trinity loan out Noah Booth to Championship new boys Hunslet]</ref>

Booth was to spend the entire season in 2025 on loan at Hunslet in the [[2025 RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].<ref>[https://hunsletrlfc.com/team/hunslet-snap-up-young-wakefield-star-on-season-long-loan/ Hunslet snap up young Wakefield star on season long loan]</ref>

[[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] engaged in a swap-deal, with Booth and fellow {{rlp|wg}}er [[Neil Tchamambe]] moving in the opposite directions.<ref>[https://hellorayo.co.uk/greatest-hits/west-yorkshire/news/wakefield-trinity-tchamambe Winger Neil Tchamambe signs new deal with Wakefield Trinity]</ref> Booth was on a season-long loan to Championship side Hunslet and the arrangement remained in place, but was able to train with Rovers during the week, allowing the coaching to start working on his development immediately.<ref>[https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-wakefield-trinity-swap-9901961 Hull KR and Wakefield Trinity set for immediate player swap deal]</ref>
